title: The Danger of DELETE 💣
description: How to not destroy your entire database.

slides.title: The Red Button 🚨
slides.content:
# The terrifying reality

Just like `UPDATE`, if you run a `DELETE` statement without a `WHERE` clause... it systematically vaporizes every single row in the entire table.

`DELETE FROM users;`

Congratulations, you just destroyed the company.

quizData.question: What happens if you run `DELETE FROM users;` without a WHERE clause?

options.text: Every single piece of data inside the users table is irrevocably wiped out.

quizData.explanation: The database assumes you know what you are doing. Without limits, it executes the command fully and completely.

slides.title: The Soft Delete alternative 🛡️
slides.content:
## A safer approach

In modern apps, developers rarely ever actually use `DELETE`.

Instead, they add a column to their tables called `is_deleted` (which defaults to False / 0).

When a user 'deletes' their account, the app actually just runs an `UPDATE`: 
`UPDATE users SET is_deleted = 1 WHERE id = 10;`

This "hides" the user without destroying data permanently! This is called a **Soft Delete**.

quizData.question: What is a 'Soft Delete'?

options.text: Deleting data but keeping a backup file on a USB drive
Avoiding the dangerous DELETE command by simply UPDATING a status column (like 'is_deleted') to hide the row instead

quizData.explanation: Soft deleting is essentially just toggling a light-switch off using UPDATE. It's wildly safer because you can just flip it back on if it was an accident.

slides.title: Perform a Soft Delete!
slides.content: Instead of deleting employee #34, safely use `UPDATE` to change their `is_active` status to `false`.
